_meta.purpose: Scene safety directives keyed by environment dropdown selection. Field AI's safety_note.ppe must be a SUPERSET of the matched entry's ppe (add only, never remove). safety_note.perimeter_m must be >= matched perimeter_m (increase only).
_meta.content_provenance: AI-generated content aligned with ERG 2024 isolation distances, OSHA HAZWOPER 1910.120 PPE Level definitions, and INSARAG operational safety guidance. NOT a substitute for actual HazMat / USAR team scene assessment.
ref_categories_used: ERG 2024 — Emergency Response Guidebook (PHMSA, public domain, https://www.phmsa.dot.gov/hazmat/erg/emergency-response-guidebook-erg)
OSHA HAZWOPER 1910.120 — PPE Level A/B/C/D definitions (public domain)
INSARAG Methodology — USAR scene safety (UN OCHA, public)

ppe_level_quick_reference.Level A: Fully encapsulating chemical-resistant suit + SCBA. Highest protection. Unknown high-concentration vapor.
ppe_level_quick_reference.Level B: SCBA + chemical-resistant suit (not encapsulating). High respiratory but lesser skin. Known agent, vapor present.
ppe_level_quick_reference.Level C: Air-purifying respirator (APR) + chemical-resistant clothing. Known agent, concentration measured, APR appropriate.
ppe_level_quick_reference.Level D: Standard work uniform + safety glasses + gloves + helmet. No respiratory hazard.

clear.directive: Standard scene safety. Maintain awareness of vehicle traffic and bystanders. Establish working perimeter with cones or tape if available.

structural_collapse_active.directive: DO NOT enter active collapse zone. Treat at perimeter. Listen for warning sounds (shifting, dust falls). Wait for USAR / heavy rescue clearance before crossing collapse line. Marker patient locations from outside.

structural_collapse_stabilized.directive: Operate within stabilized footprint marked by USAR. Listen for shifting debris. Have a designated escape route. Stand by to withdraw if structure shifts. Two-rescuer minimum.

ppe: Turnout gear or equivalent fire-resistant clothing
fire_active.directive: Do not enter fire zone without trained fire suppression personnel. Treat upwind / uphill of fire. Watch for radiant heat injury and CO/cyanide exposure even outside flame zone. Beware of compromised structures.

ppe: Level C minimum: APR with appropriate cartridge + chemical-resistant suit
chemical_suspected.directive: STOP CARE if not in appropriate PPE. Retreat upwind, uphill. Await HazMat team with atmospheric monitoring. ALL responders + patients require decontamination before further treatment. Do not eat / drink / smoke in suspected zone.

ppe: Level B: SCBA mandatory + chemical-resistant suit
chemical_known_chlorine.directive: Chlorine: dense gas, settles in low areas. Move to high ground upwind. Patients with respiratory symptoms — high-flow O2 only, do not intubate prophylactically (laryngospasm risk). Decon: water rinse + soap. Watch responders for symptom onset.
chemical_known_chlorine.ref_category: ERG 2024 Guide 124 + CDC Chlorine Fact Sheet

ppe: Level B: SCBA mandatory + chemical-resistant suit
chemical_known_ammonia.directive: Ammonia: lighter than air but condenses in moisture. Severe eye + respiratory irritant. Decon with copious water for eyes (15+ min). Patients with airway symptoms — high-flow O2, watch for delayed pulmonary edema.
chemical_known_ammonia.ref_category: ERG 2024 Guide 125 + CDC Ammonia Fact Sheet

ppe: Level A: fully encapsulating + SCBA (immediately dangerous to life and health)
chemical_known_hydrogen_sulfide.directive: H2S: dense, settles in low areas. Smell paralysis at low concentrations — sense of smell unreliable. Loss of consciousness on first breath at high concentration. Multiple-casualty incident with sudden collapse pattern suggests H2S. Withdraw if any responder reports headache / nausea.
chemical_known_hydrogen_sulfide.ref_category: ERG 2024 Guide 117 + NIOSH H2S Pocket Guide

ppe: Level B: SCBA mandatory + standard clothing
chemical_known_carbon_monoxide.directive: CO is odorless, colorless. Confined spaces especially dangerous. Multiple casualties from same enclosed space strongly suggests CO. High-flow O2 (100%) for all suspected exposure. Pulse oximetry UNRELIABLE in CO poisoning (use SpCO if available).
chemical_known_carbon_monoxide.ref_category: CDC CO Fact Sheet + AHA Toxicology

flood.directive: Water rescue is high-risk for rescuers. Throw / reach / row / go — go is last resort with trained water rescue personnel only. Watch for hidden submerged hazards (open manholes, debris, downed power lines). Decontaminate after exposure to flood water (sewage, chemicals).
flood.ref_category: FEMA Water Rescue + USCG SAR Manual

dark_low_visibility.directive: Maintain visual contact with team. Use buddy system. Mark entry / exit path with chem-lights or reflective markers. Vehicle accident scenes — set out triangles / flares well upstream of working area.
dark_low_visibility.ref_category: PHTLS 10th Night Operations + NIOSH Confined Space
